TSLA: Ross Gerber Slams Tesla's FSD For Being 'Level 2' As Gary Black Decries Marketing Strategy: 'You Still Basically Have...'. Investor Ross Gerber criticized Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, stating it's still a "Level 2" system according to SAE standards, requiring driver attention. He emphasized the need for further improvements before it can be considered truly autonomous. Fellow investor Gary Black attributed the FSD's challenges to a lack of awareness due to insufficient marketing, despite believing it's a "great product." Tesla CEO Elon Musk countered by comparing autonomous vehicles to automated elevators, suggesting that public skepticism will decrease as the technology becomes more commonplace.

Tesla ends Model S and Model X production, only a limited inventory is left. Tesla has officially announced the end of Model S and Model X production, with only a limited inventory remaining for purchase. The company is offering discounts on slightly used vehicles and special perks like free Premium Connectivity, Full Self-Driving, and Supercharging for life on remaining inventory cars. This move is part of Tesla's push towards an autonomous future, as these models paved the way for their technological advancements.

Weekend Round-Up: Tesla Triumphs Amid EV Sales Slump, Ford Faces Recall Woes And BYD Plays The Bond Card. This automotive industry recap highlights Tesla's surprising market share gain in US EV sales despite a sector-wide decline. Ford is facing ongoing recall issues with over 250,000 SUVs, while BYD is expanding into Europe with a James Bond-themed advertising campaign to challenge Tesla. Additionally, GM's shares rallied due to falling crude prices, which are boosting the auto demand outlook.

Elon Musk's Tesla Sees Q1 Slowdown—365K Deliveries Expected. Tesla is projected to deliver 365,645 vehicles in Q1 2026, marking a 12.5% decline from Q4 2025 but an 8% year-over-year increase. Analysts also anticipate over 1.6 million deliveries for 2026 and more than 3 million by 2030, alongside significant battery deployment growth. The company is buzzing with speculation about a new three-row model, even as some investors express concerns about Tesla's pivot towards AI and potential mergers with SpaceX.

Six Figure Bonuses, Ads in Maps, iPhone Parts From Bosch And More: This Week In Appleverse. This article summarizes Apple's busy week, highlighting several significant developments. These include Apple's decision to discontinue six-figure bonuses to retain iPhone talent, its collaboration with Bosch and TDK for US iPhone parts, and plans to introduce advertising in its Maps application. The piece also mentions the launch of the new MacBook Neo and remarks from an investor urging Elon Musk to create a brand identity for Tesla.

Vanguard’s VCR ETF carries 40% Amazon and Tesla exposure dressed as consumer discretionary. The Vanguard Consumer Discretionary ETF (VCR) is highlighted for its concentrated exposure to Amazon and Tesla, which together make up approximately 40% of its holdings. This significant weighting in just two growth-oriented stocks raises questions about the ETF's true diversification as a consumer discretionary fund. Investors are cautioned to be aware of this concentrated risk, as the performance of VCR will heavily depend on these two companies.

Q&A: Toyota exec Templin talks goals for "Tesla killer" EVs. Mark Templin, Toyota Motor North America's EVP and COO, discusses Toyota's strategy to significantly increase its U.S. electric vehicle market share from under 2% to 15%, aligning with its overall market presence. He refers to their upcoming BEVs as "Tesla killers" and emphasizes the company's long-term business plans over short-term political policies. Templin also highlights Toyota's portfolio approach to powertrains, including gas, hybrid, and BEVs, and their commitment to building where they sell.

Tesla Says Semi Battery Is 'Designed To Last A Million Miles'. Tesla has announced that the battery for its Semi truck is designed to last a million miles, directly addressing a major concern for electric trucking adoption related to vehicle lifespan and battery degradation. The company emphasizes the Semi's economic benefits, including lower operating costs and a nearly 20% lower total cost of ownership nationally, alongside advanced charging capabilities and improved aerodynamic efficiency. Despite past production delays, Tesla reports strong demand and significant real-world mileage logged by its test fleet, with efforts to integrate battery power for auxiliary functions to further reduce emissions and costs.

Tesla (TSLA) shares up after Jim Cramer said it had options. Shares of Tesla (TSLA) rose after Jim Cramer commented on the company's options. No further details are available regarding the nature of these options or Cramer's specific statements.

Vanguard's VCR ETF Carries 40% Amazon and Tesla Exposure Dressed as Consumer Discretionary. The Vanguard Consumer Discretionary ETF (VCR) is facing scrutiny as Amazon and Tesla constitute nearly 40% of its portfolio, leading it to behave more like a technology fund than a true consumer discretionary play. Despite a 230% ten-year return, VCR is down 9% year-to-date, primarily due to the underperformance of these two tech-heavy giants. This concentration risk and Tesla's volatility mean investors seeking traditional consumer spending exposure might be getting unintended tech exposure and volatility.

Gene Munster Says Upcoming Tesla Deliveries Will Be 'First Good Read,' As Ford, GM Scale Back EV Ambitions— Here's Why - BYD (OTC:BYDDF), BYD (OTC:BYDDY). Gene Munster of Deepwater Asset Management stated that Tesla's first-quarter delivery report, expected in early April, will be crucial for understanding underlying EV demand after the expiration of U.S. tax credits. He believes declining EV investment from legacy automakers like Ford and GM presents a significant opportunity for Tesla, especially in the U.S. market where BYD isn't a competitor. Tesla is projected to deliver 365,645 vehicles in Q1, demonstrating an 8% year-over-year increase, with long-term growth driven by high-volume models and autonomy.

YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.3879. The YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (TSLY) has announced a weekly distribution of $0.3879 per share for its shareholders. This distribution reflects the fund's strategy to generate income from options on Tesla stock.
TGT: $72.3M deal gives Federal Realty more control of major retail hub in Maryland. Federal Realty Investment Trust has acquired the remaining 18.2% interest in the Montrose Crossing shopping center in Rockville, Maryland, for $72.3 million. This acquisition brings the company's ownership to 100% and is part of its strategy to consolidate control over key properties. Montrose Crossing is a significant retail hub with over 350,000 square feet of space, anchored by Target and Giant Food, and sees strong performance with high foot traffic and sales.

All Target locations in US state to be hit with new $24 policy. Rhode Island is considering three bills that could raise the state's minimum wage to as high as $24 per hour by 2027, impacting major retailers like Target. While proponents argue this would provide a living wage and stimulate the economy, businesses and lobbyists fear it could lead to higher prices, inflation, and job losses, damaging the state's economic competitiveness. The current minimum wage in Rhode Island is $16 per hour and is set to increase to $17 next year even without the new legislation.

Target Corp. Stock: Institutional Confidence Builds as Pallas Capital Advisors Boosts Stake by 80% Ahead of Q1 Earnings. Pallas Capital Advisors LLC significantly increased its stake in Target Corp. (NYSE: TGT) by 80.2% in Q4 2025, adding 9,376 shares to reach 21,068 shares valued at $2.06 million, indicating strong institutional confidence. This move comes ahead of Target's Q1 2026 earnings report on May 15, where the retailer is expected to highlight progress in its turnaround strategy, supported by its omnichannel approach, private-label brands, and strategic initiatives in technology and sustainability. Target's financial guidance, dividend commitment, and resilience in the competitive retail landscape make it an attractive consideration for North American portfolios.

Generate Investment Management Ltd Sells 10,000 Shares of Walmart Inc. $WMT. Generate Investment Management Ltd reduced its stake in Walmart Inc. by 14.3% in Q4, selling 10,000 shares and retaining 60,000 shares valued at approximately $6.68 million. This comes amidst other institutional investor adjustments and notable insider selling from EVP John D. Rainey and Director C. Douglas McMillon. Despite these sales, Walmart reported beating Q4 EPS and revenue estimates, provided positive FY2027 EPS guidance, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" analyst consensus with an average target price near $135.76.

Exchange Traded Concepts LLC Reduces Stake in Walmart Inc. $WMT. Exchange Traded Concepts LLC has reduced its stake in Walmart Inc. by 6.3%, selling 13,716 shares and now holding 202,706 shares valued at $22.58 million. Despite this, analysts generally maintain a bullish outlook with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a price target of $135.76, citing positive digital initiatives and a disciplined buyback program. However, the company faces risks from internal insider selling by executives and potential governance issues related to a state fine and a shareholder probe into driver pay practices.

Costco Rose 7% Over the Past 6 Months. Here’s Why Its Membership Model Still Points to Upside in 2026. Costco Wholesale Corporation's stock has risen 7% over the past six months, trading near $984 per share, driven by strong comparable sales growth, increasing membership fee income, and consistent traffic. The company's unique membership-based model, which generates high-margin income, provides predictable and resilient earnings, differentiating it from peers. A valuation model suggests a target price of $1,210, implying a 23% upside over approximately 2.4 years, indicating the stock is modestly undervalued.

Dollar Tree Refocuses On Multi Price Stores And Single Banner Growth. Dollar Tree (DLTR) is expanding its multi-price strategy to include items priced up to $7 and focusing on a single banner after selling Family Dollar. The company plans 400 new store openings and 75 closures in 2026. This strategic shift aims to improve margins, traffic patterns, and its position in the discount retail sector, with the stock currently trading below analyst targets and its estimated fair value.

Vanguard (TGT) amends 13G/A: disaggregation, reports 0 shares. Vanguard Group has filed an Amendment No. 14 to its Schedule 13G/A for Target Corp (TGT), reporting zero beneficial ownership. This filing indicates an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, which causes certain Vanguard subsidiaries to report their holdings separately, as per SEC Release No. 34-39538. The amendment clarifies that this is an administrative reallocation of reporting responsibilities, not a trade, and Vanguard itself no longer holds beneficial ownership over these securities.

Target Jumps 3% During Intraday Rally — What’s Driving the Upward Trend?. Target (TGT) shares surged over 3% during an intraday rally, driven by bullish technical signals and increased options trading, with the stock challenging resistance near $121.38. The retail sector also saw positive momentum, with Walmart (WMT) experiencing gains. Despite strong short-term bullish indicators, backtesting revealed that buying TGT after a 3% intraday gain historically resulted in disappointing returns, highlighting potential risks for traders.
SBUX: Director Marissa Mayer gets stock grant at Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX). Starbucks director Marissa Mayer has received a stock award of 3,667 common shares, increasing her direct holdings to 6,041 shares. This grant, classified as a non-cash award, consists of restricted stock units granted on March 25, 2026, which are fully vested but deferred until her departure from the board. The transaction is detailed in a recent Form 4 SEC filing and includes 48 deferred stock units from a dividend reinvestment plan.

Starbucks (SBUX) director receives 3,667 fully vested deferred stock units. Starbucks director Daniel Servitje was granted 3,667 fully vested deferred stock units on March 25, 2026, as reported in a Form 4 SEC filing. These units are deferred until his departure from the board under the company's Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors. Including this award and dividend reinvestments, Servitje now directly holds 11,187 shares of Starbucks.

Director at Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) gets 2,265-share grant. Starbucks director Dambisa F. Moyo received a grant of 2,265 shares of common stock as restricted stock units on March 25, 2026. These units were fully vested at the time of grant under the company's non-employee director compensation plan. Following this award, Moyo directly holds 3,615 Starbucks shares, with the transaction being a compensation-related grant rather than an open-market purchase.

Neal Mohan (SBUX) receives 3,667 fully vested Starbucks stock units. Starbucks director Neal Mohan has received an equity award of 3,667 fully vested shares of Starbucks common stock through restricted stock units. These units, granted on March 25, 2026, are deferred until his termination of service as a director under Starbucks’ Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors. Following this award, Mohan's direct holdings in Starbucks total 11,187 shares, which includes 204 deferred stock units from reinvested dividends.

Starbucks (SBUX) director G. Michael Sievert receives 3,667-share grant. Starbucks director G. Michael Sievert was granted 3,667 shares of common stock on March 25, 2026, as fully vested restricted stock units deferred under the company's Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors. This compensation-related grant increased Sievert's direct holdings in Starbucks to 10,878 shares. The transaction is considered standard board compensation rather than a discretionary purchase or sale.

Starbucks (SBUX) director Zhang Wei receives 3,667 fully vested stock units. Starbucks director Zhang Wei received 3,667 fully vested restricted stock units on March 25, 2026, as part of her compensation, which are deferred under the company's plan for non-employee directors. This transaction increased her direct holdings to 12,376 shares, including 236 deferred stock units from dividend reinvestment. The stock award involved no cash purchase, aligning with standard director compensation practices.

Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) director granted 3,937 fully vested deferred RSUs. Starbucks director Beth Ford received a compensation award of 3,937 fully vested restricted stock units (RSUs) on March 25, 2026. These RSUs are deferred until she leaves the board under the company's Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors. After this grant, Ford's total Starbucks holdings, including reinvested dividends, amount to 14,638 shares.

Vanguard disaggregates Starbucks holdings (SBUX) after Jan 12 realignment. The Vanguard Group has filed an amendment to its Schedule 13G/A, indicating a reorganization and reporting 0 shares of Starbucks (SBUX) as beneficially owned. This change follows an internal realignment on January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report beneficial ownership separately, as per SEC guidance. Consequently, The Vanguard Group, Inc. no longer claims beneficial ownership over securities held by these disaggregated subsidiaries.

Six-figure Starbucks jobs surface for planned Nashville office. Starbucks has begun posting job openings, including some six-figure positions, for its new corporate operations office planned for Nashville later this year. Multiple sources suggest the office could be as large as 250,000 square feet, potentially located at Peabody Union. The company is relocating roles from Seattle and actively hiring locally, as revealed by job listings circulated on March 27.

Starbucks Corp. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors. Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) shares fell 4.83% on Friday to $86.81, experiencing a second consecutive day of losses. This underperformance occurred during a broader rough trading session where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw significant declines.
DIS: Citizens initiates fuboTV stock coverage at outperform on Disney deal. Citizens has initiated coverage on fuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) with a Market Outperform rating and a $13.00 price target, citing its transformative combination with Hulu + Live TV. This deal is expected to provide Fubo with greater negotiating leverage, improved content packaging, and efficient growth through Disney's ad sales and distribution. Recent developments for Fubo also include a 1-for-12 reverse stock split and varied analyst ratings from Needham, B.Riley, and Seaport Global Securities after its merger with Disney's Hulu Live.

Disneyland Paris Unveils Disney Adventure World and Opens World of Frozen, Marking a New Era of Immersive Storytelling. Disneyland Paris has unveiled its reimagined second park, now named Disney Adventure World, and officially opened World of Frozen, marking a significant expansion. This transformation aims to offer fully immersive experiences inspired by Disney Animation, Pixar, and Marvel stories, with Adventure Way serving as a gateway to new culinary and entertainment offerings, leading to Adventure Bay and World of Frozen. The inauguration featured a heartfelt moment with Make-A-Wish children and a spectacular ceremony attended by executives including Josh D’Amaro, CEO of The Walt Disney Company, and various celebrities.

The Walt Disney Company $DIS Shares Sold by Chesley Taft & Associates LLC. Chesley Taft & Associates LLC reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 9.2% in Q4 2025, selling over 15,000 shares. Despite this, analyst sentiment remains largely positive with a "Moderate Buy" rating, although recent negative headlines related to AI partnerships and gaming ventures have introduced selling pressure. Disney's stock traded around $92.50 after modestly beating quarterly EPS and revenue estimates, with parks identified as a strong core business.

$Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special$ Hits Superstar Numbers Driving 6.3 Million Views on Disney+ and Hulu. The "Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special" achieved massive success, garnering 6.3 million views on Disney+ and Hulu within three days and boosting catalog viewing by nearly 1,000%. The celebration extended beyond streaming, influencing pop culture through widespread user-generated content, brand collaborations, and significant social media engagement across multiple platforms. Miley Cyrus's new song and original Hannah Montana tracks also saw substantial increases in streaming, underscoring the franchise's enduring popularity.

Disneyland Paris Welcomes President Macron for the Unveiling of Disney Adventure World, a Transformative Reimagining of the Resort's Second Park. Disneyland Paris welcomed French President Emmanuel Macron for the unveiling of Disney Adventure World, a significant expansion representing a €2 billion investment and nearly doubling the park's footprint. This reimagining, which includes the new "World of Frozen" land, is set to open on March 29 and is expected to create 1,000 new jobs and boost tourism. An independent study highlighted Disneyland Paris' substantial economic impact on France, contributing 6.1% to national tourism revenue and supporting 70,000 jobs.

Wedge Capital Management L L P NC Raises Position in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Wedge Capital Management L L P NC increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 12.2% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 33,519 shares, bringing their total holdings to 307,511 shares valued at approximately $34.99 million. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, surpassing revenue and EPS estimates, with analysts forecasting a 5.47 EPS for the current fiscal year. Despite some strategic concerns regarding AI partnerships, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Disney with an average target price of $134.

The Walt Disney Company Stock: Institutional Confidence Builds Amid Streaming Gains and Theme Park Strength. Institutional investors are showing increased confidence in The Walt Disney Company as it achieves streaming profitability and its theme parks deliver record revenues. Exchange Traded Concepts notably increased its stake in Q4 2025, reflecting optimism in Disney's strategic shift. The article highlights Disney's diversified business model, the accelerating path to profitability for its streaming segment, and the robust recovery of its theme parks as key drivers for long-term value for North American investors.

Generali Investments CEE investicni spolecnost a.s. Increases Holdings in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Generali Investments CEE investicni spolecnost a.s. increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 19.4% in Q4, acquiring an additional 16,642 shares to own a total of 102,398 shares valued at $11.65 million. Walt Disney now constitutes 0.8% of Generali Investments CEE's holdings, making it their 25th largest position. Despite beating quarterly earnings estimates, Disney's stock is down approximately 2.4%, facing mixed analyst sentiment and strategic headwinds such as the cancellation of the OpenAI "Sora" partnership.

Vanguard Amendment shows 0% stake in Walt Disney Co (NYSE: DIS). The Vanguard Group filed an amendment (Schedule 13G/A) reporting zero beneficial ownership of Walt Disney Co common stock. This change is due to an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, where certain subsidiaries will now report their beneficial ownership separately in accordance with SEC guidelines. The amendment emphasizes that this is a structural reporting change, not a sale of Disney shares.

General Mills Bets On Moana Cereal Tie In And Clean Label Push. General Mills has launched two new cereals, Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney’s Moana, both utilizing natural colors as part of its clean label initiative. This move aims to increase visibility and family engagement, despite the company's shares experiencing a significant decline over the past one, three, and five years. The strategy underscores General Mills' efforts to revitalize its cereal division through brand reinvestment, licensing partnerships, and reformulated ingredients, while balancing potential impacts on margins.

Nike World Cup Waste Textiles Test Investor Faith In Turnaround. Nike has launched a 2026 World Cup apparel line made from recycled textiles, aiming to link sustainability with a major sporting event as part of its brand turnaround efforts. This initiative is under investor scrutiny regarding its potential to boost brand perception, demand, and higher-quality revenue, especially given Nike's recent stock performance and existing challenges like margin pressures. While it could differentiate Nike from competitors and increase brand loyalty among sustainability-focused consumers, risks include production costs, potential discounting, and consumer reception impacting inventory and financial strain.
